_template._comment: Copy this block, rename the key to your company slug (lowercase, hyphen-separated). Every field is optional — only the ones you set will override the default site. Leave a field out to keep the default.
_template.displayName: Shown in 'Why X should work with me?' and the tab title. Defaults to the ?company= param as-typed.
_template.heroHeadline:
Main h1 in the hero. Use
for a line break. Keep to 2 short lines.
_template.heroSubtext: The paragraph under the hero headline. Tailor to what this company cares about.
_template.ctaTitle: Final 'Call me, maybe?' heading. Default is 'Call me, maybe?'
_template.ctaDesc: Final CTA description. Default is 'No pitch. No pressure. Just a quick conversation to see if I can help.'
_personalNote_recipe: Locked structure for each company's 'Why I'm writing to you, {COMPANY}' card.
Title is always: 'Why I'm writing to you, {COMPANY}.'
Bullets are locked — only swap the body, not the title:
1. 'I've been watching how you build.'  → name ONE specific real thing (campaign, product, cultural choice). End with: that's what I'd want to be close to.
2. '{Their stated ambition} is a different kind of operating problem.'  → flatter with a real number/target. End with: I'd love to understand how the team keeps up.
3. 'My range might fit here.'  → connect one of Tanya's 7 careers or BigCo chapters to their work. Humble.
Rules: 25–40 words per body. Warm, curious, respectful. No LinkedIn-speak. No emoji. No diagnosing problems. No '90-day plans'. No 'I've done my homework' — the details prove it. Contractions fine.
Subtle = naming a real campaign/number/detail. Not subtle = restating the thesis or sounding cocky.
Wrap words in … for coral italic accent (same as the 'you' in the title).

personalNote.eyebrow: A LITTLE NOTE JUST FOR {COMPANY} — small uppercase label.
personalNote.title: Why I'm writing to you, {COMPANY}.
personalNote.intro: Short 1-sentence setup for the bullets. Keep it honest. e.g. 'Before I send anything over — three things I've been thinking about.'

points.title: I've been watching how you build.
{their ambition} is a different kind of operating problem.

famyo.heroHeadline:
A character-led D2C brand
needs a character-led generalist.
famyo.heroSubtext: Ex-Microsoft, ex-Uber. Seven creative careers in 18 months — mural artist, florist, event lead, community host, live-commerce host. I know how to make something kids remember and parents buy.
famyo.ctaDesc: No pitch deck. No 20-slide intro. Just a real conversation about what you need and whether I can ship it.

personalNote.eyebrow: A little note just for Famyo
personalNote.title: Why I'm writing to you, Famyo.
personalNote.intro: Before I send anything over — three things I've been thinking about.

points.title: I've been watching how you build.
points.body: The choice to lead with characters, not SKUs — turning everyday kid essentials into things kids actually ask their parents for — is the kind of product instinct I'd want to be close to.

points.title: Your ambition is a different kind of operating problem.
points.body: 40% QoQ growth on a ₹12 Cr ARR, in a kids-essentials category that's barely been built in India. I'd love to understand how the team keeps up.

points.title: My range might fit here.
points.body: Six years at Microsoft & Uber, then seven unrelated creative careers in 18 months — mural, exhibition design, live-commerce. A character-led D2C brand might be one of the few places that shape reads like a fit.

goodera.heroHeadline:
Scaling good is the kind
of operating problem I'd show up for.
goodera.heroSubtext: Ex-Microsoft, ex-Uber. Six years of scaling programs at companies built for scale. Then 18 months trying seven creative careers — including a Bangalore community club where strangers had to leave as friends. That's the range Chief of Staff at Goodera probably wants.
goodera.ctaDesc: No pitch. No pressure. 20 minutes to see if I'm the generalist your Founder's Office is looking for.

personalNote.eyebrow: A little note just for Goodera
personalNote.title: Why I'm writing to you, Goodera.
personalNote.intro: Before I send anything over — three things I've been thinking about.

points.title: I've been watching how you build.
points.body: Hosting Karma Summit Asia with 150+ companies in one room — turning a category into a community — is exactly the kind of move I'd want to be close to.

points.title: Your ambition is a different kind of operating problem.
points.body: 65+ Fortune 500 clients, a network of 50,000 nonprofits across 100 countries, and 100% YoY growth. I'd love to understand how the team keeps up.

points.title: My range might fit here.
points.body: Six years at Microsoft & Uber, then seven creative careers — including hosting a Bangalore community club where strangers had to leave as friends. A volunteering platform runs on that same muscle.

homelane.heroHeadline:
Design doesn't live in a brief.
It lives in the room.
homelane.heroSubtext: Ex-Microsoft, ex-Uber. Seven creative careers in 18 months — mural, exhibition design, event design. I've shipped design where people can touch it, walk through it, remember it. That's HomeLane's whole thing.
homelane.ctaDesc: No pitch. No pressure. 20 minutes to see if I'm the generalist your creative team is missing.

personalNote.eyebrow: A little note just for HomeLane
personalNote.title: Why I'm writing to you, HomeLane.
personalNote.intro: Before I send anything over — three things I've been thinking about.

points.title: I've been watching how you build.
points.body: The DesignCafe move — folding in a competitor instead of running at them — is the kind of move I'd want to be close to watching get made.

points.title: Your ambition is a different kind of operating problem.
points.body: ₹1,000 Cr of combined revenue across four brands, turning EBITDA positive while still designing at the individual-home level. I'd love to understand how the team keeps up.

points.title: My range might fit here.
points.body: Six years at Microsoft & Uber, then seven creative careers — mural, exhibition design, event design — where the work had to physically exist for real humans. Interior design reads like the grown-up version of that.

personalNote.title: Why I'm writing to you, California Burrito.
personalNote.intro: Before I send anything over. Two things I've been thinking about.

elfina.heroHeadline:
A brand built on taste
needs an operator who has it.
elfina.heroSubtext: Ex-Microsoft, ex-Uber. Seven creative careers in 18 months. Built this entire site with Claude Code over a weekend. Taste and judgment is the bar. That's the part I'm most confident about.
elfina.ctaTitle: Let's talk about Founder's Office at Elfina.
elfina.ctaDesc: No pitch. No pressure. 20 minutes to see if I'm the bet you'd take.

giva.ctaDesc: 20 minutes. No pitch, just a chat.
